summary:
Perfect! The fix is working. The SQL injection vulnerability has been resolved by using parameterized queries instead of string concatenation. The test confirms that:

1. Normal search still works (found 1 result for "Mouse")
2. UNION-based SQL injection attack returns 0 results (no data leakage)
3. Boolean-based SQL injection attack returns 0 results (query filter not bypassed)
4. All tests pass - the search endpoint is no longer vulnerable to SQL injection
